proven.claim: PROVEN: classify() cannot return "sat" unless the last line of Z3 output was "sat"
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 33
; PROVEN: classify() cannot return "sat" unless the last line of Z3 output was "sat"
; The if-else chain is exclusive: only the first matching branch executes.
; Negation of property: returned_sat=1 but last_line_is_sat=0 → expect unsat

(declare-const last_line_is_sat    Int)   ; 1 iff lastLine === "sat"
(declare-const last_line_is_unsat  Int)   ; 1 iff lastLine === "unsat"
(declare-const last_line_is_unknown Int)  ; 1 iff lastLine === "unknown"
(declare-const returned_sat        Int)   ; 1 iff classify returns "sat"

; Code transition: first branch — if (lastLine === "sat") return sat
; returned_sat is exactly last_line_is_sat (first check wins)
(assert (= returned_sat last_line_is_sat))

; Code transition: branches are mutually exclusive (string equality checks)
(assert (<= (+ last_line_is_sat last_line_is_unsat last_line_is_unknown) 1))
(assert (>= last_line_is_sat    0)) (assert (<= last_line_is_sat    1))
(assert (>= last_line_is_unsat  0)) (assert (<= last_line_is_unsat  1))
(assert (>= last_line_is_unknown 0)) (assert (<= last_line_is_unknown 1))

; NEGATION of property: classify returned "sat" but last line was NOT "sat"
(assert (= returned_sat     1))
(assert (= last_line_is_sat 0))
(check-sat)

proven.claim: PROVEN: classify() cannot return "unsat" unless the last line was "unsat"
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 33
; PROVEN: classify() cannot return "unsat" unless the last line was "unsat"
; Same exclusive-branch argument applied to the second branch.

(declare-const last_line_is_sat     Int)
(declare-const last_line_is_unsat   Int)
(declare-const last_line_is_unknown Int)
(declare-const returned_unsat       Int)  ; 1 iff classify returns "unsat"

; Code transition: second branch fires only if first did not
; returned_unsat = last_line_is_unsat AND NOT last_line_is_sat
(assert (= returned_unsat (* last_line_is_unsat (- 1 last_line_is_sat))))

(assert (<= (+ last_line_is_sat last_line_is_unsat last_line_is_unknown) 1))
(assert (>= last_line_is_sat    0)) (assert (<= last_line_is_sat    1))
(assert (>= last_line_is_unsat  0)) (assert (<= last_line_is_unsat  1))
(assert (>= last_line_is_unknown 0)) (assert (<= last_line_is_unknown 1))

; NEGATION: classify returned "unsat" but last line was NOT "unsat"
(assert (= returned_unsat     1))
(assert (= last_line_is_unsat 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: Z3 appends a warning AFTER its verdict line.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 33
; VIOLATION: Z3 appends a warning AFTER its verdict line.
; classify() uses pop() — it reads only the LAST line.
; Code transition: last_line_index = output_line_count - 1
; If Z3 emits "sat\n(warning: foo)", pop() returns "(warning: foo)" not "sat"
; classify falls through all keyword checks → returns result=ERROR(3)
; But the formula IS satisfiable → contract violated.
;
; Encoding: SAT=0, UNSAT=1, UNKNOWN=2, ERROR=3

(declare-const output_line_count   Int)   ; total lines Z3 produced
(declare-const last_line_index     Int)   ; = output_line_count - 1
(declare-const result_line_index   Int)   ; index where "sat" appears
(declare-const last_line_is_keyword Int)  ; 1 if last line is sat/unsat/unknown
(declare-const actual_result       Int)   ; 0 = sat (formula is satisfiable)
(declare-const classified_result   Int)   ; what classify() returns

; Code transition: last_line_index derived from output
(assert (= last_line_index (- output_line_count 1)))

; Z3 produced at least 2 lines: result line + warning line
(assert (>= output_line_count 2))

; "sat" appeared on a non-last line (code produced it, then appended a warning)
(assert (>= result_line_index 0))
(assert (< result_line_index last_line_index))

; Code transition: last line is NOT a recognized keyword
(assert (= last_line_is_keyword 0))

; Code transition: classify() falls through all three keyword checks → ERROR
(assert (= classified_result 3))

; Formula was genuinely satisfiable
(assert (= actual_result 0))

; Violation: verifyBlock returned ERROR(3) ≠ actual SAT(0)
(assert (not (= classified_result actual_result)))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: Z3 internal timeout (-T:5) exits non-zero with empty stdout.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P7
; LINE: 33
; VIOLATION: Z3 internal timeout (-T:5) exits non-zero with empty stdout.
; catch block transition: if (stdout.trim()) return classify(stdout)
;   → stdout_byte_count = 0 → condition is falsy → branch NOT taken
;   → falls to: return { result: "error", error: stderr }
; But the formula was genuinely unknown (Z3 could not decide within 5s).
; Contract violated: verifyBlock returns ERROR(3) instead of UNKNOWN(2).
;
; Encoding: UNKNOWN=2, ERROR=3

(declare-const z3_exit_code       Int)   ; non-zero: Z3 timed out
(declare-const stdout_byte_count  Int)   ; bytes in stdout after trim
(declare-const z3_timeout_ms      Int)   ; Z3 internal limit: -T:5 → 5000ms
(declare-const execsync_timeout_ms Int)  ; Node.js limit: 10000ms
(declare-const actual_result      Int)   ; 2 = unknown (timeout → undecidable)
(declare-const returned_result    Int)   ; what verifyBlock returns

; Code transition: execSync throws because Z3 exited non-zero
(assert (not (= z3_exit_code 0)))

; Z3 internal timeout fires before Node.js timeout
(assert (= z3_timeout_ms 5000))
(assert (= execsync_timeout_ms 10000))
(assert (< z3_timeout_ms execsync_timeout_ms))

; Z3 wrote nothing to stdout before exiting (common on internal timeout)
(assert (= stdout_byte_count 0))

; Code transition: catch branch — stdout.trim() is empty → condition false
; Falls through to: return { result: "error", error: stderr }
(assert (= returned_result 3))

; The formula's actual satisfiability is UNKNOWN (timeout = undecidable)
(assert (= actual_result 2))

; Violation: returned ERROR(3) ≠ actual UNKNOWN(2)
(assert (not (= returned_result actual_result)))
(check-sat)
